title: Trachelospermum fragrans
intro: Trachelospermum fragrans, also known as Fragrant Mile-a-Minute, is a flowering evergreen vine native to China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Trachelospermum fragrans contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, capsules, and ointments.
taxonomy: Trachelospermum fragrans is a flowering evergreen climber in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to China and Taiwan. It belongs to the genus Trachelospermum and is a member of the order Gentianales. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The species Trachelospermum fragrans is further classified as a type of flowering plant in the class Magnoliopsida.

Some studies suggest that extracts from the plant have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. benefits: Trachelospermum fragrans, also known as the fragrant Jessamine, is a climbing evergreen plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. preparations: Trachelospermum fragrans is a plant that has been used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. side_effects: Trachelospermum fragrans is not commonly associated with severe side effects, but like many plants, it can cause mild skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals.
